methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $1.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 20.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Substrate is a Series A chip design company founded by James and Oliver Proud, currently valued at $1.0B+ post-money following $100M+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 3 public semiconductor names (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private frontier-chip firms including Rain AI, Etched, and Cerebras at similar or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods apply to this target. Stage-progression anchors to Substrate's $1.0B last post-money valuation and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A progression, implying $2.0B. Peer-median analysis examines 3 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Rain AI at $500M (Series A), Positron AI at $1.0B (Series B), and Etched at $5.0B (Series B)—yielding a median of $1.0B and a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (20.3x median across the three public names) cannot be applied because Substrate's revenue is not disclosed. Across the two applicable methods, the triangulated range is $850M (low) to $2.3B (high), with a median of $1.5B.

The median estimate of $1.5B sits between current valuation and the stage-progression upside case. Key uncertainties include product commercialization velocity, competitive positioning relative to Etched and similar peers, and the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($500M–$5.0B), which reflects sector heterogeneity in funding environment and perceived technological differentiation.
